item.name: Adamantine Arrow
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object using this ammunition, the hit is a critical hit.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Adamantine Arrows (20)
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object using this ammunition, the hit is a critical hit.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Adamantine Blowgun Needle
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object using this ammunition, the hit is a critical hit.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Adamantine Blowgun Needles (50)
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object using this ammunition, the hit is a critical hit.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Adamantine Crossbow Bolt
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object using this ammunition, the hit is a critical hit.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Adamantine Crossbow Bolts (20)
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object using this ammunition, the hit is a critical hit.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Adamantine Sling Bullet
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object using this ammunition, the hit is a critical hit.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Adamantine Sling Bullets (20)
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object using this ammunition, the hit is a critical hit.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Adamantine Battleaxe
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, battleaxe Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Club
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, club Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Dagger
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Proficiency: simple, dagger Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Flail
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Proficiency: martial, flail Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Glaive
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.
Reach: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: martial, glaive Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Greataxe
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: martial, greataxe Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Greatclub
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: simple, greatclub Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Greatsword
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: martial, greatsword Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Halberd
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.
Reach: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: martial, halberd Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Handaxe
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Proficiency: simple, handaxe Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Javelin
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Proficiency: simple, javelin Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Lance
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Special: You have disadvantage when you use a lance to attack a target within 5 feet of you. Also, a lance requires two hands to wield when you aren't mounted.
Reach: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.
Proficiency: martial, lance Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Light Hammer
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Proficiency: simple, light hammer Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Longsword
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, longsword Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Mace
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Proficiency: simple, mace Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Maul
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: martial, maul Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Morningstar
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Proficiency: martial, morningstar Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Pike
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.
Reach: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: martial, pike Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Quarterstaff
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: simple, quarterstaff Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Rapier
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Proficiency: martial, rapier Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Scimitar
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: martial, scimitar Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Shortsword
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: martial, shortsword Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Sickle
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, sickle Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Spear
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: simple, spear Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Trident
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, trident Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine War Pick
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Proficiency: martial, war pick Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Warhammer
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, warhammer Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Whip
item.text:
Whenever you hit an object with this weapon, the hit is a critical hit.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Reach: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.
Proficiency: martial, whip Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Adamantine Breastplate
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Adamantine Chain Mail
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 13, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Adamantine Chain Shirt
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Adamantine Half Plate Armor
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Adamantine Plate Armor
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Adamantine Ring Mail
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Adamantine Scale Mail
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Adamantine Splint Armor
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 78, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Breastplate of Gleaming
item.text:
This armor never gets dirty.
This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Chain Mail of Gleaming
item.text:
This armor never gets dirty.
Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 13, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Chain Shirt of Gleaming
item.text:
This armor never gets dirty.
Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Half Plate Armor of Gleaming
item.text:
This armor never gets dirty.
Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Hide Armor of Gleaming
item.text:
This armor never gets dirty.
This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts. It is commonly worn by barbarian tribes, evil humanoids, and other folk who lack access to the tools and materials needed to create better armor.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Plate Armor of Gleaming
item.text:
This armor never gets dirty.
Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Ring Mail of Gleaming
item.text:
This armor never gets dirty.
This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Scale Mail of Gleaming
item.text:
This armor never gets dirty.
This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Splint Armor of Gleaming
item.text:
This armor never gets dirty.
This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Cast-Off Breastplate
item.text:
You can doff this armor as an action.
This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Cast-Off Chain Mail
item.text:
You can doff this armor as an action.
Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 13, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Cast-Off Chain Shirt
item.text:
You can doff this armor as an action.
Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Cast-Off Half Plate Armor
item.text:
You can doff this armor as an action.
Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Cast-Off Hide Armor
item.text:
You can doff this armor as an action.
This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts. It is commonly worn by barbarian tribes, evil humanoids, and other folk who lack access to the tools and materials needed to create better armor.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Cast-Off Leather Armor
item.text:
You can doff this armor as an action.
The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by being boiled in oil. The rest of the armor is made of softer and more flexible materials.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Cast-Off Padded Armor
item.text:
You can doff this armor as an action.
Padded armor consists of quilted layers of cloth and batting.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Cast-Off Plate Armor
item.text:
You can doff this armor as an action.
Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Cast-Off Ring Mail
item.text:
You can doff this armor as an action.
This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Cast-Off Scale Mail
item.text:
You can doff this armor as an action.
This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Cast-Off Splint Armor
item.text:
You can doff this armor as an action.
This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Cast-Off Studded Leather Armor
item.text:
You can doff this armor as an action.
Made from tough but flexible leather, studded leather is reinforced with close-set rivets or spikes.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 136, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Moon-Touched Greatsword
item.text:
In darkness, the unsheathed blade of this sword sheds moonlight, creating bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: martial, greatsword Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 138, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Moon-Touched Longsword
item.text:
In darkness, the unsheathed blade of this sword sheds moonlight, creating bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, longsword Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 138, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Moon-Touched Rapier
item.text:
In darkness, the unsheathed blade of this sword sheds moonlight, creating bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Proficiency: martial, rapier Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 138, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Moon-Touched Scimitar
item.text:
In darkness, the unsheathed blade of this sword sheds moonlight, creating bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: martial, scimitar Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 138, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Moon-Touched Shortsword
item.text:
In darkness, the unsheathed blade of this sword sheds moonlight, creating bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: martial, shortsword Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 138, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 149

item.name: Smoldering Breastplate
item.text:
Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.
This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Smoldering Chain Mail
item.text:
Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.
Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 13, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Smoldering Chain Shirt
item.text:
Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.
Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Smoldering Half Plate Armor
item.text:
Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.
Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Smoldering Hide Armor
item.text:
Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.
This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts. It is commonly worn by barbarian tribes, evil humanoids, and other folk who lack access to the tools and materials needed to create better armor.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Smoldering Leather Armor
item.text:
Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.
The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by being boiled in oil. The rest of the armor is made of softer and more flexible materials.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Smoldering Padded Armor
item.text:
Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.
Padded armor consists of quilted layers of cloth and batting.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Smoldering Plate Armor
item.text:
Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.
Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Smoldering Ring Mail
item.text:
Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.
This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Smoldering Scale Mail
item.text:
Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.
This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Smoldering Splint Armor
item.text:
Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.
This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 145

item.name: Smoldering Studded Leather Armor
item.text:
Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.
Made from tough but flexible leather, studded leather is reinforced with close-set rivets or spikes.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 144

item.name: Walloping Arrow
item.text:
This ammunition packs a wallop. A creature hit by the ammunition must succeed on a DC 10 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Walloping Arrows (20)
item.text:
This ammunition packs a wallop. A creature hit by the ammunition must succeed on a DC 10 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Walloping Blowgun Needle
item.text:
This ammunition packs a wallop. A creature hit by the ammunition must succeed on a DC 10 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Walloping Blowgun Needles (50)
item.text:
This ammunition packs a wallop. A creature hit by the ammunition must succeed on a DC 10 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Walloping Crossbow Bolt
item.text:
This ammunition packs a wallop. A creature hit by the ammunition must succeed on a DC 10 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Walloping Crossbow Bolts (20)
item.text:
This ammunition packs a wallop. A creature hit by the ammunition must succeed on a DC 10 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Walloping Sling Bullet
item.text:
This ammunition packs a wallop. A creature hit by the ammunition must succeed on a DC 10 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150

item.name: Walloping Sling Bullets (20)
item.text:
This ammunition packs a wallop. A creature hit by the ammunition must succeed on a DC 10 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: Xanathar's Guide to Everything p. 139, Player's Handbook (2014) p. 150
